title: carex gracilis subsp. corynophora
intro: Carex gracilis subsp. corynophora, also known as the slender sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It is a perennial sedge native to Australia, and traditionally used by indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties. The plant is reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may provide health benefits such as wound healing and protection against infections. In herbal preparations, Carex gracilis subsp. corynophora can be made into a tea or infused oil, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
